 {"id":"bd-22gc","title":"Deep exploration: SLB (Simultaneous Launch Button)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of SLB (Simultaneous Launch Button) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify SLB installation
[[ -d /dp/simultaneous_launch_button ]] && echo "PASS: slb repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: slb repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v slb &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: slb command available" || { echo "FAIL: slb not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check if slb daemon/server is available
pgrep -f slb 2>/dev/null && echo "INFO: slb process running" || echo "INFO: slb not currently running"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/slb-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/simultaneous_launch_button/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for two-person rule docs, coordination protocol

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Two-person rule implementation
  - Confirmation mechanism (how both agents confirm)
  - Timeout handling for confirmations
  - Integration with destructive command protection
  - Coordination protocol (messaging, locks)

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
slb --help 2>&1 | head -20
slb confirm --help 2>&1 | head -10
slb status --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
slb status 2>&1 | head -5
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'slb OR simultaneous launch OR two-person rule'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'slb coordination' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/simultaneous_launch_button/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/simultaneous_launch_button && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Two-person rule mechanism
- [ ] Confirmation protocol and timeout
- [ ] Command types requiring two-person approval
- [ ] Integration with dcg (destructive command guard)
- [ ] Recovery from failed confirmations

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] dcg - destructive command protection
- [ ] mail - confirmation messaging
- [ ] ntm - multi-agent coordination

### 2.3 Safety Verification
```bash
# Check what commands trigger two-person rule
cat /dp/simultaneous_launch_button/config/*.yaml 2>/dev/null | head -20
# Or check code for command patterns
grep -r "dangerous\|destructive" /dp/simultaneous_launch_button/src/ 2>/dev/null | head -10
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update slb entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Two-person rule for dangerous commands
- `description`: Coordination protocol
- `deepDescription`: How confirmation works
- `features`: Verified safety capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations (dcg, mail, ntm)

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test slb entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const slb = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'slb');
console.log('Testing slb entry...');
console.assert(slb, 'slb entry exists');
console.assert(slb.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(slb.connectsTo?.includes('dcg'), 'connects to dcg');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Coordination Flow (Safe Mode)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/slb-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== SLB E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: Status check
echo "Test 1: Status check..." | tee -a $LOG
slb status 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Help for confirm
echo "Test 2: Confirm help..." | tee -a $LOG
slb confirm --help 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 3: List pending (if available)
echo "Test 3: Pending operations..." | tee -a $LOG
slb pending 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No pending command"

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-22gc --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Safety mechanisms documented correctly
- [ ] Synergies are reciprocal (slb->dcg and dcg->slb)
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:01:23.124119983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:14:24.854921039Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:14:24.854835038Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-29fl","title":"acfs status: One-line health summary command","description":"## Overview
Add `acfs status` command that outputs a single-line health summary, perfect for shell prompts or quick checks.

## Current Problem
- `acfs doctor` is verbose and takes time to run
- No quick way to check "is everything OK?"
- Shell prompt integration requires parseable output

## Proposed Output Format
```bash
$ acfs status
ACFS OK: 26 tools, last update 2h ago

$ acfs status  # with issues
ACFS WARN: 2 outdated tools, doctor recommended
```

## Exit Codes
- 0: Everything healthy
- 1: Warnings (outdated tools, minor issues)
- 2: Errors (missing tools, broken state)

## Implementation Details
1. New file: `scripts/commands/status.sh`
2. Quick checks only (no network calls by default):
   - State file exists and valid
   - Required tools present in PATH
   - Last update timestamp
3. Optional `--check-updates` for network-based checks

## Machine-Readable Mode
```bash
$ acfs status --json
{"status":"ok","tools":26,"last_update":"2026-01-25T21:00:00Z","warnings":[]}
```

## Shell Prompt Integration
```bash
# Add to .bashrc
acfs_status() { acfs status --short 2>/dev/null || echo "?"; }
PS1='$(acfs_status) \w $ '
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test exit codes for all states
- [ ] Test --json output is valid JSON
- [ ] Test runs in < 100ms (no network)
- [ ] Test with missing state file
- [ ] Test with outdated state

## Files to Create
- scripts/commands/status.sh","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:26.052120972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:41:34.694666041Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:41:34.694647606Z","close_reason":"Implemented scripts/lib/status.sh with --json, --short, --check-updates modes. Updated acfs.zshrc routing and help text.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-29fl","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:03:25.805296607Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2a8k","title":"ACFS update should self-update first","description":"The acfs update command should update ACFS itself (git pull) BEFORE updating any other components. Currently no self-update happens.

Critical because:
1. Bug fixes in update.sh won't apply without manual git pull
2. Security fixes in checksums.yaml won't apply
3. New tools in manifest won't be discovered

Fix: Add update_acfs_self() as FIRST operation that does git fetch/pull and re-execs if update.sh changed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T21:16:03.928848634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T21:46:31.334103730Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T21:46:31.334032095Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critical","self-update","update"]}
 {"id":"bd-2bg3","title":"Deep exploration: CAAM (Coding Agent Account Manager)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of CAAM (Coding Agent Account Manager) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify CAAM installation
[[ -d /dp/coding_agent_account_manager ]] && echo "PASS: caam repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: caam repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v caam &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: caam command available" || { echo "FAIL: caam not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check supported CLI tool availability
command -v claude &>/dev/null && echo "INFO: claude CLI available"
command -v codex &>/dev/null && echo "INFO: codex CLI available" || echo "INFO: codex not installed"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/caam-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/coding_agent_account_manager/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for account configuration docs, supported profiles

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Account storage mechanism (encrypted? plaintext?)
  - Switching implementation (sub-100ms claim)
  - CLI profiles supported (Claude, GPT, Gemini, Codex)
  - Rate limit detection strategy
  - Environment variable management
  - Config file location and format

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
caam --help 2>&1 | head -20
caam list --help 2>&1 | head -10
caam switch --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
caam list 2>&1 | head -10
```

### 1.4 Performance Claim Verification
```bash
# Verify sub-100ms switching claim
echo "Testing switch timing..."
time (caam switch test-profile 2>/dev/null || echo "No test profile") 2>&1
```

### 1.5 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'caam OR account manager OR rate limit'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'caam account switching' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.6 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/coding_agent_account_manager/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/coding_agent_account_manager && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Account storage format (file location, encryption)
- [ ] Switching mechanism and actual timing
- [ ] Supported CLI profiles (complete list)
- [ ] Rate limit detection method
- [ ] Environment management approach

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] ntm - multi-agent account coordination
- [ ] slb - coordinated account switching
- [ ] mail - account status messaging

### 2.3 Claim Verification
```bash
# Verify sub-100ms claim with timing
START=$(date +%s%N)
caam list >/dev/null 2>&1
END=$(date +%s%N)
ELAPSED=$(( (END - START) / 1000000 ))
echo "caam list took: ${ELAPSED}ms"

# Check config location
ls -la ~/.config/caam/ 2>/dev/null || echo "Config location differs"
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update caam entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Accurate one-liner (verify sub-100ms claim)
- `description`: Account management capabilities
- `deepDescription`: How switching actually works
- `features`: Verified account operations
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test caam entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const caam = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'caam');
console.log('Testing caam entry...');
console.assert(caam, 'caam entry exists');
console.assert(caam.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(caam.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Account Operations
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/caam-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== CAAM E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: List accounts
echo "Test 1: List accounts..." | tee -a $LOG
caam list 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Current account
echo "Test 2: Current account..." | tee -a $LOG
caam current 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No current command"

# Test 3: Help for add
echo "Test 3: Add help..." | tee -a $LOG
caam add --help 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-2bg3 --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Performance claims verified (sub-100ms)
- [ ] Content matches verified tool capabilities
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:01:18.982295810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:27:47.367564776Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:27:47.367538427Z","close_reason":"CAAM deep exploration complete: verified 50+ commands, added project-profile associations, caam run automatic failover, pick command with fzf","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2cz8","title":"Deep exploration: APR (Automated Plan Reviser Pro)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of APR (Automated Plan Reviser Pro) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.
